field_description: The broadest service grouping, comparable to an economic sector. Service Sector, Service Domain, and Service Family form a hierarchy.

field_option.option_description: Medical, clinical, rehabilitation, mental health, spa, wellness, fitness, and animal-care services.
Beauty, grooming, domestic support, family care, lifestyle, spiritual, and intimate wellness services.
Construction, trade, repair, cleaning, landscaping, property, and security services.
Vehicle, passenger, freight, roadside, maritime, and aviation services.
Food preparation, dining, catering, accommodation, travel, and event services.

Manufacturing support, equipment maintenance, agriculture, forestry, fisheries, mining, laboratory, and warehousing services.

field_description: A mid-level service grouping inside a Service Sector. option_parent_code references the parent group_service_sector code.

Strategy, management, operations, and specialist consulting.
Hiring, staffing, HR, training coordination, and workforce services.
Advertising, promotion, branding, campaign, and market services.

Software development, hosting, systems, cybersecurity, and IT support.
Voice, data, internet, network, and communication services.
Consumer electronics, appliance, device, and technical repair.
Audio, video, broadcast, animation, and production services.
Graphic, web, industrial, visual, and communication design.
Photography, videography, editing, and visual documentation.
Writing, editing, publishing, localization, and content services.
Data processing, records, analytics, information, and knowledge systems.
Formal school, college, university, and academic instruction.
Trade, technical, occupational, and competency-based training.
Individual or group academic, skills, and performance coaching.
Examination, competency assessment, certification, and licensing support.
Research, library, archival, investigative, and information services.
Language teaching, translation, interpretation, and language support.
Visual art, craft, museum, cultural, and heritage services.
Music, theater, dance, performance, and live entertainment services.
Leisure, amusement, hobby, outdoor, and recreation services.
Sports instruction, officiating, facility, competition, and athletic service.
Video-game, tabletop, esports, and interactive entertainment services.
Government transactions, licensing, administration, and civic services.

Funeral, cremation, burial, memorial, and bereavement services.
Electricity, gas, telecommunications utility, and public utility operations.
Water supply, wastewater, sanitation, drainage, and sewer services.

Collection, recycling, recovery, disposal, and material diversion.
Environmental assessment, remediation, conservation, and compliance.

selection_rule: Single primary Service Family per normalized service
field_description: The specific classification level assigned directly to service_main records. The options below are the current Service Families used by Massage Nexus; they are reference taxonomy records under the broader hierarchy rather than the complete list of every service family in the economy. option_sector_code and option_domain_code reference the parent hierarchy codes.
classification_note: The source document presents Service Sector and Service Domain with explicit field names but does not state a field name for the Service Family level; the field name group_service_family is inferred from the documented hierarchy naming pattern and requires confirmation.
Massage is the primary family when a service is commercially and publicly presented principally as massage.
Manual Bodywork is used when presenting the service merely as massage would materially misrepresent its method, professional tradition, or client expectation.
Reflexology is organized around reflex maps or zones and remains distinct from ordinary massage of the same body area.
Hydrotherapy and Aquatic Bodywork requires water, buoyancy, water pressure, immersion, or aquatic movement to function as a central treatment method.
Bathing and Wet Wellness centers on bathing, soaking, bathhouse access, or a wet-wellness experience rather than a structured therapeutic water method.
Thermal and Recovery Wellness centers on heat, cold, compression, or recovery exposure rather than water itself.
Device-Led Wellness is used when specialized equipment is the principal delivery mechanism and the service is not more accurately a regulated medical or aesthetic procedure.
Medical Aesthetic Service remains separate when medical supervision, regulated practice, or medical-aesthetic scope is central.
Massage Nexus may later adopt another existing domain or add a new family record without changing the common hierarchy fields or code length.

field_option.option_description: Hands-on or tool-assisted manipulation presented primarily as massage.
Pressure-based service organized around reflex maps or zones.
Hands-on body-focused work not best represented as ordinary massage.
Assisted stretching, mobility, joint movement, and range-of-motion service.
Water, pressure, buoyancy, immersion, or aquatic movement as the central method.
Heat, cold, compression, and recovery-focused wellness service.
Bathing, soaking, bathhouse, wet-wellness, and communal bathing experience.
Wellness service delivered primarily through specialized equipment or a device.
Guided relaxation, breathwork, meditation, sound, Reiki, and related non-massage practices.
Wellness intake, evaluation, planning, consultation, and guidance.
Scrub, polish, wrap, mask, mud, exfoliation, and comparable body care.
Facial cleansing, skin care, facial treatment, and related non-medical service.
Manicure, pedicure, hand spa, foot spa, nail treatment, and related care.
Haircut, styling, hair treatment, scalp care, and salon-based hair service.
Barbering, shaving, waxing, threading, sugaring, and related grooming.
Medically supervised or regulated aesthetic procedure.
Licensed or regulated clinical, rehabilitation, pelvic-health, or therapeutic treatment.
Consensual sensual or erotic bodywork whose primary presentation involves arousal-oriented touch.
Consensual intimate service presented primarily for sexual-wellness education, exploration, or stimulation.
Direct adult sexual activity represented for moderation, safety, legal handling, and accurate classification.

field_description: Classifies one alias of a normalized service record.
classification_note: A translated display label is dynamic translated content rather than automatically a service alias.
A provider-specific branded menu name is not a service alias unless it is independently used or recognized beyond that provider.

field_option.option_description: Widely used alternative name for the same service.
Name primarily associated with a country, region, culture, or market.
Name used for the service in a particular source language, locality, or linguistic community.
Name converted from one writing system to another.
Shortened form or recognized acronym.
Earlier name retained for history and discovery.
Frequent misspelling retained only for search and correction.
Former label no longer preferred.

selection_rule: One relationship type per connected service pair
field_description: Classifies the relationship between two normalized service records. option_inverse_map defines the strict automatic inverse mapping.
classification_note: Directional inverse relationships must strictly follow option_inverse_map when automatically generated.

For the CLN and SEN inverse pair: a sensual, sexual-wellness, or adult service pointing to its clinical counterpart uses CLN; a clinical service pointing to its sensual, sexual-wellness, or adult counterpart uses SEN.

field_option.option_description: Source service is broader than the connected service.
Source service is narrower than the connected service.
Source service is the direct parent of the connected service.
Source service is the direct child of the connected service.
Both records represent the same service concept.
Services overlap substantially but are not fully interchangeable.
Services have a meaningful non-hierarchical relationship.
Services are frequently mistaken for one another.
Source service may be included as a component of the connected service.
Services are commonly performed or sold together.
Connected service is the regulated or clinical counterpart.
Connected service is the sensual, sexual-wellness, or adult counterpart.
Source service replaces an older normalized service identity.
Source service has been replaced by a newer identity.
Source record was consolidated into the connected canonical record.
Source service is required or recommended before the connected service.
Source service naturally follows or is performed after the connected service.

selection_rule: Single-select per establishment_service or practitioner_service document
field_description: Classifies the commercial structure of one provider menu item.
classification_note: A Package bundles separately identifiable services, products, facilities, or components.
A Series repeats substantially similar sessions.
A Course follows an ordered or progressive program.
Menu variants may differ by duration, price, body focus, client count, practitioner count, room, location, or another selectable condition.
Promotions are time-bound commercial rules and belong to promotion records rather than permanent menu identity.

field_option.option_description: One provider-specific service offering.
One session blending several normalized services or systems.
One purchase containing several separately identifiable components.
One purchase containing repeated or substantially similar sessions over time.
An ordered or progressive multi-session program whose sessions may develop toward a defined objective.
Optional supplement attached to another menu item.
Paid or included use of a facility with or without treatment.

field_description: Distinguishes structured systems, cultural traditions, named methods, and services defined mainly by purpose. It supports discovery, filtering, and educational content without replacing the normalized service record in service_main.

field_option.option_description: Organized massage method with recognizable principles and session logic.
Culturally or historically rooted massage or healing practice.
Recognized named approach not necessarily constituting a complete tradition.
Service primarily defined by intended outcome, treatment goal, or philosophical approach rather than a named system or tradition.

selection_rule: Multi-select where several supported levels apply
field_description: Supported massage pressure levels. Selecting several levels represents the supported pressure range.
classification_note: Pressure adaptation to preference, tolerance, feedback, body area, or session need is provider-operational information rather than a pressure level.

field_option.option_description: Minimal controlled pressure for cautious or highly sensitive care.
Gentle pressure commonly used for comfort and relaxation.
Moderate pressure between gentle and firm work.
Strong controlled pressure below the provider's deepest range.
Deepest controlled pressure appropriate to the service and client.

field_description: The generic medium involved in massage contact. A named or branded product is linked through the service-product relationship and Product Classification; it is not a Massage Medium option.
classification_note: Specific carrier oils, ingredients, scents, essential-oil blends, CBD, magnesium, warming agents, cooling agents, and similar formulation details belong to product records or provider add-ons rather than this broad medium field.

field_option.option_description: No glide product is used.
Massage oil is the principal medium.
Lotion is the principal medium.
Cream is the principal medium.
Balm or ointment is used throughout or on selected areas.
Gel is the principal or targeted medium.
Powder supports glide or a traditional method.
Water itself is materially involved in treatment contact.

selection_rule: Multi-select where several supports are normally used
field_description: The physical support normally used during treatment. Portability describes service deployment rather than treatment support and is not included in this field.
classification_note: Aquatic characteristics are represented through Service Family, Water Contact Method, and Floatation Support rather than a separate Aquatic Setup option.
Provider menu items or variants hold the authoritative operational support used for an actual booking.

field_option.option_description: Purpose-built table used to support massage or treatment.
Purpose-built treatment bed or padded treatment platform.
Mat or padded floor support.
Seated massage or treatment support.
Ordinary sleeping bed used as the treatment support.
Reclining chair or supported reclined seat.
Float, buoyancy aid, aquatic cradle, or comparable water support.
Hammock, sling, aerial support, or suspended treatment setup.

field_description: Actual client positions used during the service. This field contains actual positions only.

field_option.option_description: Client lies face down.
Client lies face up.
Client lies on one side.
Client remains seated.
Client rests in a reclined or semi-reclined position.
Client stands during all or part of the service.
Client is supported principally on one or both knees.
Client is supported in a hands-and-knees or comparable four-point position.

field_description: How client positioning normally behaves during the service.

field_option.option_description: The service normally remains in one principal position.
The service normally changes among two or more defined positions.
Guided, assisted, or active movement is central to positioning.

selection_rule: Multi-select where several garments may be used
field_description: Client attire normally used during the service.

field_option.option_description: The client wears normal everyday clothing during the service.
A loose, traditional, reusable, or purpose-specific garment is used.
A single-use undergarment, top, spa wrap, or treatment garment is used.
The client retains their own underwear.

field_description: Level of client undressing in the normal service setup.

field_option.option_description: Normal clothing or a complete treatment garment remains in place.
Clothing is removed only from selected treatment areas.
The client removes all clothing for the service where lawful, consensual, and appropriate.

selection_rule: Multi-select where several contact arrangements apply
field_description: How treatment contact is applied relative to clothing and skin.

field_option.option_description: Contact is applied through clothing or a treatment garment.
Contact is applied directly to exposed skin.
The service intentionally combines over-clothing and direct-skin contact.

field_description: Whether and how professional draping is used in the normal service setup. Clothing, undressing, skin contact, and draping do not by themselves determine whether a service is clinical, professional, sensual, or adult.

field_option.option_description: Professional draping covers areas not currently being treated.
Draping may be selected according to consent, context, law, and provider policy.
The service is normally performed without draping in an appropriate lawful and consensual context.
Clothing, treatment garments, service format, or facility practice makes draping unnecessary.

field_description: The generic item used for draping. Fiber, material, dimensions, absorbency, construction, reusability, and actual branded items belong to Product Classification.

field_option.option_description: A sheet is used as the principal draping item.
One or more towels are used for draping.
A blanket is used for coverage, warmth, or draping.
A spa wrap or comparable body wrap is used for draping.

selection_rule: Single-select per menu variant or session
field_description: How many clients receive the service together.

field_option.option_description: One client receives the service.
Two clients receive concurrent or paired services.
Three or more clients receive an organized service.

selection_rule: Single-select per menu variant or session
field_description: How many practitioners deliver the service to one client.

field_option.option_description: One practitioner serves one client.
Two practitioners serve one client.
More than two practitioners participate.

field_description: Broad cross-system physical action grouping for named massage techniques. Technique records use four levels: Technique Framework, Technique Group, Named Technique, and Technique Variation. Technique Framework and Named Technique are reference records rather than fixed field options; see massage_technique_framework.json.
classification_note: Reiki is a normalized non-massage service under Mind-Body and Relaxation. It is not a Massage Technique Framework or Massage Technique Group.

field_option.option_description: Continuous sliding or stroking contact.
Compression with lifting, squeezing, rolling, or grasping.
Focused rubbing or friction across selected tissue.
Rhythmic striking, tapping, hacking, cupping, or pounding.
Rapid or rhythmic shaking and oscillating movement.
Static or rhythmic pressure without required glide.
Focused pressure applied to selected points or pathways.
Assisted or guided lengthening.
Controlled passive or assisted joint movement.
Repetitive rhythmic movement of the body or segment.
Controlled pulling or lengthening force.
Sustained or guided work directed toward fascia.
Focused work on localized areas of tenderness or referred discomfort.
Technique performed with a linked manual tool, product, or instrument.
Heat or cold is applied as part of the technique.
Technique is performed through water support, buoyancy, or aquatic motion.

selection_rule: Multi-select per named technique or normalized service where applicable
field_description: Identifies the practitioner body part materially used to apply touch, pressure, movement, stimulation, support, or other direct contact during a named technique or service. Sensitive or intimate contact values are valid neutral taxonomy data; public visibility, age gating, moderation, legality, payment handling, and booking eligibility remain separate policy decisions.
classification_note: Selections must follow the documented technique or normalized-service definition. Do not infer contact body parts merely from a suggestive service name.
This field describes the practitioner's contact body part. The part of the client receiving the service belongs to a separate client body-area or service-target classification.

field_option.option_description: One or more fingers are used for contact or manipulation.
The thumb is used as the principal contact body part.
The palm provides broad contact, pressure, or glide.
The whole hand is used without requiring a more specific hand part.
The side or ulnar edge of the hand is used.
One or more knuckles are used.
A closed or partially closed fist is used.
The wrist or wrist area is used for contact or pressure.
The forearm is used for gliding, compression, or pressure.
The elbow is used for focused or deeper pressure.
The arm is used more broadly than the forearm alone.
The shoulder is used as a contact or pressure area.
The chest is used as part of direct consensual body contact.
The breast is used as part of direct consensual body contact.
The abdomen is used as part of direct consensual body contact.
The back is used as part of direct consensual body contact.
The hip is used for contact, positioning, or movement.
The buttocks are used as part of direct consensual body contact.
The thigh is used for contact, pressure, or body movement.
The knee is used for supported pressure or compression.
The leg is used as a broader contact body part.
The foot is used for pressure, glide, or supported contact.
The heel of the foot is specifically used.
The mouth is used as part of direct consensual contact.
The lips are specifically used for direct consensual contact.
The tongue is used as part of direct consensual contact.
The penis is used as part of direct consensual intimate contact.
The vulva is used as part of direct consensual intimate contact.
The scrotum is used as part of direct consensual intimate contact.
Broad body-to-body contact is central and cannot be represented accurately by one isolated body part.

selection_rule: One relationship type per service-to-product-type or service-to-product connection
field_description: Records the generic role of a product in a service. Product Classification records the physical item, formulation, material, specifications, brand, version, and actual product identity.

field_option.option_description: The product type is necessary for the defined method or service identity.
The product type is commonly used but not universally required.
The product type may be selected without changing the service identity.
The product type may substitute for another accepted product type.
The product adds an optional feature, sensation, or commercial enhancement.
The product type has a documented historical or cultural association with the service.

field_description: Body area targeted by a reflexology service. Several selected targets already represent a combined reflexology service; a separate Combination option is unnecessary.

field_option.option_description: Reflexology focused on the feet.
Reflexology focused on the hands.
Reflexology focused on the ears.
Reflexology focused on the face.

field_description: How water contacts the client during a water-based service. Water Contact Method does not classify dry-sauna or infrared-sauna exposure.

field_option.option_description: Client is partly or fully immersed in water.
Water is delivered through a shower or overhead flow.
Pressurized water jets provide the principal action.
Buoyancy and movement in water are central.
Passive or guided soaking is central.
Water is used directly without full immersion.

field_description: Water temperature levels used in the service.

field_option.option_description: Intentionally high-temperature water exposure.
Comfortably warm water below hot treatment level.
Approximately neutral or body-comfort temperature.
Cool water exposure above intense cold level.
Deliberate cold-water exposure.

field_description: How temperature behaves across the service.

field_option.option_description: One principal temperature range is maintained.
Hot and cold exposures alternate or are intentionally combined.
Temperature changes according to the session without a fixed contrast sequence.

field_description: Non-water thermal exposure methods. Steam, dry sauna, and infrared sauna remain distinct methods.

field_option.option_description: Humid heat or vapor exposure is central.
Heated low-humidity room exposure is central.
Infrared radiant heat exposure is central.

selection_rule: Single-select per bathing offer or facility context
field_description: How clients access a bathing facility.

field_option.option_description: Bathing facility is reserved for one booking party.
Several unrelated clients may use the same facility.
Group bathing is a defining social or cultural format.
Access is shared through timed or rotating admission.

field_description: Actions performed during a body treatment.

field_option.option_description: Removes surface soil, residue, or product.
Removes or loosens dead surface skin.
Smooths and refines the skin surface.
Applies a treatment layer for a defined period.
Encloses or covers the body or selected area.
Focuses on moisture and skin comfort.
Applies a heated, cooled, herbal, or product-based compress.
Includes a required rinse or wash stage.

field_description: The generic medium defining a body treatment. Exact ingredients, formulation, allergens, warnings, brand, and product version belong to Product Classification.

field_option.option_description: Mud is a defining treatment medium.
Clay is a defining treatment medium.
Salt is a defining scrub, soak, polish, or treatment medium.
Sugar is a defining scrub or polish medium.
Plant-derived material is a defining treatment medium.
Herbs or an herbal preparation are materially involved.
Algae is a defining treatment medium.
Seaweed is a defining treatment medium.
A mineral preparation is materially involved.
Cream is a defining treatment medium.
Gel is a defining treatment medium.
Oil is a defining treatment medium.

field_description: Actions performed during a facial or skin-care service.

field_option.option_description: Cleanses the skin.
Supports moisture and skin comfort.
Removes or loosens dead surface skin.
Includes manual or device-assisted extraction.
Applies a treatment mask for a defined period.
Smooths or refines the skin surface.

field_description: Skin concerns the service is presented to address.

field_option.option_description: Service is presented for acne-prone skin within permitted scope.
Service addresses age-related appearance or skin-comfort concerns.
Service is adapted for sensitive or reactive skin.
Service addresses uneven tone or pigmentation concerns.
Service focuses on skin texture and surface refinement.

field_description: How the skin-care service is principally delivered.

field_option.option_description: The service is delivered principally through manual methods.
A device materially supports the service.
Manual and device-assisted methods are both material.

field_description: Target of a nail, hand, or foot care service.

field_option.option_description: Hand or fingernail service.
Foot or toenail service.
Nail plate, enhancement, or polish-centered service.
Surrounding hand, foot, cuticle, or callus care.

field_description: Actions performed during a nail service.

field_option.option_description: Cleans the nail or surrounding area.
Cuts or shortens nails.
Files or shapes nails.
Treats or maintains cuticles.
Applies ordinary polish.
Applies gel-based polish.
Adds artificial length or enhancement.
Repairs damaged natural or artificial nails.
Removes polish, gel, extension, or enhancement.
Treats or reduces callused skin.

field_description: Target of a hair and scalp care service. Selecting both Hair and Scalp represents an integrated service; a separate Both option is unnecessary.

field_option.option_description: Hair length, strand, style, or condition.
Scalp cleansing, comfort, or treatment.

field_description: Actions performed during a hair service.

field_option.option_description: Cuts or reshapes hair.
Cleanses hair or scalp.
Blow-dries or otherwise dries hair.
Arranges or styles hair.
Colors, tones, highlights, or changes hair color.
Applies a conditioning or corrective treatment.
Chemically or mechanically straightens hair.
Chemically or mechanically curls hair.
Adds hair extensions or comparable enhancement.

field_description: Method used to remove hair. Barbering is a normalized grooming service rather than a Hair Removal Method; Shaving remains the applicable method when hair is removed with a razor.

field_option.option_description: Removes hair using a razor or shaving implement.
Removes hair using wax.
Removes hair using thread.
Removes hair using sugar-based paste.
Removes individual hairs mechanically.
Uses a non-medical device for hair removal.

field_description: Actions performed during a grooming service.

field_option.option_description: Shortens or shapes hair or facial hair.
Defines lines, contours, or form.
Removes hair through shaving.
Cleans the treated area.
Arranges hair or facial hair.
Applies finishing, aftercare, or presentation product.

field_description: Physical form of the principal device in a device-led wellness service.

field_option.option_description: Client uses a specialized device in chair form.
Client uses a specialized device in bed form.
Client enters or uses an enclosed or semi-enclosed chamber.
Operator or client uses a handheld powered device.
Device is worn or attached during the session.
Client stands, lies, or moves on a specialized platform.
Client uses a compact enclosed or semi-enclosed pod.

field_description: How the device or equipment is deployed for service delivery.

field_option.option_description: Equipment is permanently or semi-permanently installed.
Equipment can be transported and assembled for service delivery.
Equipment is integrated into a mobile facility or service unit.

field_description: How guidance is delivered in a mind-body service. Mind-Body services use type_client_arrangement for Individual or Group participation.

field_option.option_description: A live facilitator leads the session in real time.
Guidance is delivered through recorded media.
Participant proceeds without active facilitation.

field_description: Purpose of a wellness consultation or assessment service.

field_option.option_description: Gathers initial client information before service.
Evaluates client condition, need, or suitability.
Checks for contraindications or safety concerns.
Establishes a service or treatment plan.
Explains a service, condition, or self-care guidance.
Reviews progress or outcome after a prior service.
Directs the client toward another appropriate service or provider.

field_description: Regulatory requirement context for a service. Training, credentialing, licensure, and medical oversight may overlap and do not form one universal ordered scale. Every recorded requirement must preserve jurisdiction, source, effective date, scope, and verification context.
classification_note: Jurisdiction-dependent uncertainty belongs to jurisdiction-specific legal or professional review rather than a classification option.

field_option.option_description: Ordinary consumer service without a specific regulated professional scope in the reviewed context.
Requires provider training or demonstrated competency under an identified standard or practice context.
Requires a defined credential, certification, or recognized competency in the reviewed context.
Requires a licensed or legally authorized professional.
Requires medical direction, prescription, supervision, or facility oversight.
